name: Okinawa
description: Japan's tropical paradise — a chain of subtropical islands with crystal-clear turquoise water, world-class coral reefs, distinct Ryukyu culture, and some of the world's longest-living people.

quickInfo.visa: Visa-free for most Western countries (90 days)

hotels.name: Guestyard Okinawa
hotels.description: Charming guesthouse in Naha's Kokusai Street area, great base for exploring the island

hotels.name: Okinawa Marriott Resort & Spa
hotels.description: Beachfront resort with stunning ocean views, multiple pools, and direct beach access

hotels.name: Vessel Hotel Campana Okinawa
hotels.description: Ferris wheel views from your room, next to the lively American Village entertainment area

hotels.name: The Busena Terrace Beach Resort
hotels.description: Award-winning luxury resort on its own private peninsula with pristine coral beach

hotels.name: Halekulani Okinawa
hotels.description: Okinawa's most prestigious luxury resort, five infinity pools cascading to the ocean

activities.name: Kerama Islands Snorkeling & Diving Tour
activities.description: Boat trip to Japan's best coral reefs with crystal visibility, sea turtles guaranteed

activities.name: Shurijo Castle & Naha Old Town Walking Tour
activities.description: Explore the UNESCO Ryukyu kingdom castle and traditional Tsuboya pottery district

activities.name: Ocean Expo Park & Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium
activities.description: One of the world's largest aquariums featuring whale sharks and manta rays

activities.name: Yanbaru Jungle Trekking & Waterfall Tour
activities.description: Hike through UNESCO-listed subtropical forest to hidden waterfalls in the north

activities.name: Ryukyu Village Cultural Experience
activities.description: Try Ryukyu dance, bingata dyeing, and sanshin shamisen in a living heritage village

items.activity: Tsuboya pottery street and ceramics shopping
Kokusai Street shopping and Okinawan sweets
Orion beer and goya champuru dinner
Snorkeling over Kerama coral reefs & sea turtles
BBQ lunch on Zamami Island beach
Nakijin Castle ruins and coastal views
Drive back, dinner at American Village

practicalInfo.gettingThere: Naha Airport (OKA) has direct flights from Tokyo (2h30min), Osaka (2h), and international connections from Taipei, Hong Kong, Seoul, and Shanghai.
practicalInfo.localTransport: Yui Rail monorail covers central Naha. Car rental essential for exploring the rest of the island (~$40/day). Buses are slow. IC card: OKICA.
